prologue.title: The Debt
prologue.subtitle: Gold Coast — late 1947
prologue.historicalNote: Before the march. Before the Crossroads. Before the riots.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: They sent us to Burma, to Italy, to die for a King we never met.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: When we came home, the Treasury said the debt was settled. The ledgers said otherwise.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: In Accra, in '47, we stopped asking politely.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Dr. Nkrumah spoke of self-government — not with rifles, but with signatures and witnesses.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: On the twenty-eighth of February, we would march to Christiansborg unarmed.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: A petition. A promise kept by the people, if not by the Governor.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Sergeant Adjetey — you, Attipoe, Lamptey — you do not start a war today.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: You gather truth. You protect the march. You make sure the world is watching.

barks.kojo_order_hold: Kojo: "Araba — hold the choke! Nobody passes!"
barks.kojo_order_suppress: Kojo: "Kofi — suppressing fire! Keep their heads down!"
barks.kojo_order_flank: Kojo: "On my mark — flank left through the cut!"
barks.araba_riot_rally: Araba: "You want a riot? I'll show you a door breaking!"
barks.kofi_first_shot: Kofi: "I… I hit something! Don't tell Yaw I said that."
barks.kwesi_wiretap: Kwesi: "Lines open. I can read their patrol rhythm."
barks.adjetey_cover: Adjetey: "Down! Civilians behind us — hold fire unless they fire first!"
barks.adjetey_orders: Adjetey: "Squad — on me. Ink before iron today."
barks.adjetey_civilians: Adjetey: "Market women clear the lane — let them pass!"
barks.attipoe_contact: Attipoe: "Eyes on the ridge — two constables!"
barks.attipoe_escort: Attipoe: "Keep the VIPs between us and the road!"
barks.lamptey_charges: Lamptey: "Charges set — fuse is yours, Sergeant."
barks.lamptey_demolition: Lamptey: "Stand back — mast comes down on three!"
barks.lamptey_ready: Lamptey: "Demolition kit ready. Point me at steel, not stalls."
barks.araba_stall: Araba: "AWAM shutters — not Auntie Esi's fish!"
barks.yaw_rules: Yaw: "Hit the monopoly — not the market women."
barks.kwesi_document: Kwesi: "Witness this — file it before they file us."
barks.adjetey_low_hp: Adjetey: "Still standing — so is the march. On me!"
barks.adjetey_under_fire: Adjetey: "They want a riot — give them discipline!"
barks.adjetey_extraction_rush: Adjetey: "Extraction green — move before the cordon seals!"
barks.adjetey_hold_pressure: Adjetey: "Hold the square — the petition doesn't read itself!"
barks.attipoe_low_hp: Attipoe: "Scratch on the arm — route still open!"
barks.attipoe_escort_endangered: Attipoe: "VIP taking fire — cart between us and the lane!"
barks.kojo_low_hp: Kojo: "Bleeding's fine — the alley isn't. Move!"
barks.kojo_extraction_rush: Kojo: "Exit's lit — last sprint, no heroics!"
barks.araba_hold_pressure: Araba: "They want us gone — we plant our feet here!"
barks.kwesi_spotted: Kwesi: "Constable line of sight — I need smoke or shadow!"
barks.kofi_under_fire: Kofi: "They're shooting back — I'm still shooting!"

ambush: Attipoe: "Contact east — they're already on the radio!"
extraction: Adjetey: "Safe house in sight — thirty seconds of honest running."
Kojo: "Cordon's tightening — through the cut, now!"
handoff: Lamptey: "Satchel changes hands — eyes on the rooftops."
Kofi Asare: "One breath, Sergeant — then I'm gone."
holdZone: Araba: "Let them come — we owe the martyrs a witness!"

lines.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
lines.text: Burma took my left knee and my patience for colonial arithmetic.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
lines.text: They promised three million pounds to men who dug graves in Italy. The Treasury paid excuses.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
lines.text: Nkrumah says ink before iron. I say ledgers before lies — then we march unarmed to Christiansborg.

lines.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
lines.text: Attipoe runs hot. Lamptey runs quiet. I run the squad home alive long enough to be heard.

lines.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
lines.text: I was fastest in the training yard — fastest into the bush, fastest out of Sicily.

lines.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
lines.text: Home they called us heroes until we asked for our pay. Then we were 'agitators.'

lines.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
lines.text: Makola taught me betrayal smells like wet canvas. I still scout first — someone has to see the trap.

lines.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
lines.text: Twenty-eighth of February I'll be at Adjetey's shoulder. Unarmed. Visible. Not afraid.

lines.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
lines.text: Demolition school in Egypt — they needed Gold Coast hands for steel that wouldn't bend.

lines.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
lines.text: I don't talk much because fuses don't care about speeches.

lines.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
lines.text: Radio Hill isn't revenge — it's so their troop maps lie when we march.

lines.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
lines.text: The petition is paper. My job is making sure the Castle hears it over their own static.

lines.speaker: Kofi Asare
lines.text: I file in English. I dream in Twi. The Secretariat thinks those are different languages.

lines.speaker: Kofi Asare
lines.text: Every false route I feed them buys a true marcher one more hour.

lines.speaker: Kofi Asare
lines.text: They'll call me traitor in the market and asset at the Castle. Both are costumes.

lines.speaker: Kofi Asare
lines.text: When they arrest me at dawn, the petition copy must already be ink, not hope.

lines.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
lines.text: Self-government is not a gift from London — it is a demand we document.

lines.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
lines.text: Bonne's boycott starved their shops. Our march will starve their excuses.

lines.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
lines.text: If they answer petition with powder, let witnesses survive to testify.

lines.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
lines.text: Discipline is your weapon. The Crossroads will judge who struck first.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: I ran messages for the Union before I ran from constables — same legs, different fear.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: I counted the shots at Christiansborg. Three martyrs. One country breaking.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: Bonne cracked the shops. Imray cracked the people. I crack routes — not random rage.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: Araba breaks doors. Kwesi breaks files. I break their timing.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: AWAM raised prices while veterans bled. Bonne told us close the stalls — we did.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: My cousin Kwame marched unarmed. A second volley took his leg, not his life — this time.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: I don't whisper politics. I stand where the crowd needs a spine.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: When Kojo's trapped, I am the hammer. When innocents stall, I am the shield.

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: Yesterday I carried loads at Kaneshie. Today I carry a rifle I barely trust.

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: I stood frozen at the Crossroads while Adjetey fell. I won't stand still again.

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: Not Kofi Asare at the Secretariat — names matter when constables hunt clerks.

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: Yaw says hit AWAM, not Auntie Esi. Kojo says learn before you lead. I'm learning fast.

lines.speaker: Kwesi
lines.text: Private Odartey Lamptey was my father's cousin. I watched him fall on red earth I still see.

lines.speaker: Kwesi
lines.text: I don't carry a gun — I carry manifests they think are boring.

lines.speaker: Kwesi
lines.text: Imray signed suppression orders in my office. I photocopied them for Accra.

lines.speaker: Kwesi
lines.text: When they catch me, the Watson Commission must read what I filed — not what they burned.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: My grandfather marched on the twenty-eighth — unarmed, like Adjetey. A volley took his hip, not his memory.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: I run Ussher Yard, not front lines. Tools, routes, and rules keep children from becoming martyrs early.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: Hit AWAM stores. Protect Auntie Esi's fish. That was Bonne's boycott — that's still mine.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: Four of you go out at dusk. Four come back — or don't waste my bolts on a funeral.

phaseIvOrigin.title: The Powder Keg — How the Cell Formed
phaseIvOrigin.historicalNote: After Christiansborg Crossroads. Bonne's AWAM boycott had already strained Accra; the shootings broke the barrier of fear.

scenes.where: Christiansborg Crossroads — laterite dust still in the air

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Three names the newspapers will spell wrong by morning. Three bodies the Governor will not come down to see.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: I counted the shots. I counted again. You don't need a rifle to know when a country breaks.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: They marched with empty hands. Sergeant Adjetey. Corporal Attipoe. Private Odartey Lamptey.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: The constables reload like it's drill. The crowd scatters. Fear wins — for one hour.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: No. Fear had a long run in this city. Bonne's boycott cracked the shops. Today cracked the people.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: I know every alley from Makola to James Town. If they want a riot, I'll give them something worse — a plan.

scenes.where: Salaga Market — AWAM store shuttered, oil lamp under awning

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: Move. Unless you work for AWAM, move.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: I was at the Crossroads. I'm not police.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: Then you saw what they did to men who wore the King's uniform.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: I saw. Who's that?

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: My cousin Kwame. He marched unarmed. A second volley took his leg, not his life — this time.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: AWAM raised prices while veterans bled. Bonne told us to close our stalls. We closed them. They still shot us.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: I'm looking for people who won't swallow this.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: I'm looking for throats that won't stay quiet. What are you offering?

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: Routes. Timing. Smoke when steel fails. Not chaos — pressure.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: …When you need a door broken, find me. I don't do whisper politics.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: When I need the ground to shake, I'll know where to stand.

scenes.where: Kaneshie — smoke from AWAM warehouse, crowd running

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: The five-day fires begin. Not every hand holds a weapon. Every hand holds something — a stone, a ledger, a brother.

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: Leave me — they killed the Sergeant! They killed—

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: Down, boy. Constables at the corner.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: Grab his arm. Not the chest — the arm. Run on my count.

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: Who are you—

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: Three. Two. One — now.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: They slip through a drainage cut Kojo used since he was fifteen.

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: I was at the Crossroads. I couldn't… I just stood there.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: Standing is how most people die. You ran today. That counts.

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: I want to do more than run.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: You will. But you'll learn before you lead. What's your name?

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: Kofi. Kaneshie. I carry loads at the market.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: Not Kofi Asare at the Secretariat, eh? Good. Names matter this week.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: He stays with us until the streets cool.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: He stays until he chooses to leave. Fair?

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: …Fair.

scenes.where: Ussher Town motor yard — back room, Bedford truck on blocks

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: You brought trouble to my yard.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: We brought three hungry people and one angry city.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: Same thing, some weeks.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: We need a roof. AWAM hired constables to watch the markets.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: Sit. All of you. Especially the boy — you shake like you still hear shots.

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: I hear them.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: My grandfather marched on the twenty-eighth. Unarmed. Like Adjetey. Like Attipoe. Like Lamptey.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: He did not fall — a secondary volley took his hip and his faith in the Crown. He lived crippled. He lived angry. He lived useful.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: You run the boycott runs. The safe routes.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: I run what keeps children from becoming martyrs before their names are written. I do not march with you.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: We need more than roofs.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: You'll get tools. Oil. Bolts. A map of who owes the veterans' Union. And my grandfather's rule — hit AWAM stores, not Auntie Esi's fish table.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: We need intelligence. Police manifests. Castle schedules.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: Then you need someone who reads English without blinking. I know one clerk who didn't sleep last night.

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: Another Kofi?

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: Kwesi. Different boy. Different courage. You'll meet him when he's ready — or when the city forces his hand.

scenes.where: Victoriaborg lane — rain on corrugated iron

lines.speaker: Kwesi
lines.text: Don't step into the light.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: Yaw said you'd come alone.

lines.speaker: Kwesi
lines.text: I came with a satchel. That's worse.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: Show us or run back to your desk.

lines.speaker: Kwesi
lines.text: Private Odartey Lamptey was my father's cousin. I watched him fall on red earth I still see when I close my eyes.

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: …I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Kwesi
lines.text: Sorry doesn't read curfew times. This does.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: He slides out stolen pages — patrol rotations, warehouse keys, Superintendent Imray's own signature on a suppression order.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: You could hang for this.

lines.speaker: Kwesi
lines.text: I could hang for filing. I choose what I file.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: Can you get us inside the AWAM depot on Ring Road?

lines.speaker: Kwesi
lines.text: I can tell you which door the night guard forgets to lock. I will not carry a gun through it.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: No one asks you to. You wire the lines. You read the rooms. We move on what you know.

lines.speaker: Kwesi
lines.text: And when they catch me?

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: We come. All of us.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: I'll come first.

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: I'll… I'll learn to shoot straight by then.

lines.speaker: Kwesi
lines.text: …Then I have one condition. We document everything. Witnesses. Ledgers. Names. What Imray did at the Crossroads — the world must read it, not just hear it in market gossip.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: Agreed. Truth is ammunition.

scenes.where: Ussher Town yard — same back room, maps on crate

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: Four children and an old man's yard. The Watson Commission will come if you survive. Nkrumah will be arrested if you succeed. Independence is not this week — but the path starts here.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: We need a chain. Not a crowd.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: Say it.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: I plan. I flank. I give orders. If I'm trapped, Araba breaks the line — not the other way around.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: Second in command. The hammer. Accepted.

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: What am I?

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: You learn fast or you die slow. Suppressing fire. Cover. Courage on training wheels until the wheels come off.

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: I won't fail the three martyrs.

lines.speaker: Kwesi
lines.text: I don't fight. I don't run first into fire. I make fire unnecessary when I can.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: And I keep the yard, the tools, and the memory of my grandfather — who marched so you wouldn't have to repeat his mistakes blindly.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: For Adjetey. For Attipoe. For Lamptey. For every stall Bonne closed and every price AWAM raised.

lines.speaker: Araba
lines.text: For Kwame's leg. For the petition they never read.

lines.speaker: Kwesi
lines.text: For the file they thought I'd never open.

lines.speaker: Kofi
lines.text: For the day I stopped standing still.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: Then go. Missions start at dusk. You come back alive — all four — or don't come back at all.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Accra burns five days. They burn with purpose.

bandingOrder.how: Witnesses Crossroads alone; decides organized resistance
Kojo finds her tending wounded cousin; mutual pact
Rescued from constables during first riot fires
Offers yard as hub; refuses frontline; connects to Kwesi
Brings stolen manifests; joins on truth condition

lines.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
lines.text: Brothers — we do not ask for charity. We ask for accounts.

lines.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
lines.text: Gather the ledgers. Escort the organizers. Let the colony see we are disciplined, not desperate.

lines.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
lines.text: Ink before iron. Every pamphlet is a witness. Every witness is a step toward self-government.

lines.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
lines.text: Twenty-eighth of February. Christ Church at dawn. Two thousand unarmed veterans.

lines.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
lines.text: We march with dignity. We present a petition — loyal in tone, uncompromising in demand.

lines.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
lines.text: If they strike, let the world see who struck first. Protect the speakers. Protect the truth.

lines.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
lines.text: Christiansborg Crossroads. Governor Creasy will not come down. Superintendent Imray blocks the gate.

lines.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
lines.text: You cannot rewrite what history will record — but you can get the witnesses out alive.

lines.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
lines.text: Document everything. The Watson Commission will come if we survive to tell it.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: Crossroads dust still on your boots. My yard is open — my grandfather's rule stands.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: Hit AWAM stores. Protect innocent stalls. Kwesi reads the rooms — Kojo moves the steel.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: Independence is not this week. But every manifest you steal is a page they cannot burn.

epilogue.title: The Path to Independence
epilogue.historicalNote: Watson Commission · Nkrumah's arrest · Ghana independent 6 March 1957

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Five days Accra burned. The Watson Commission came. The Crown heard what Imray tried to silence.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Dr. Nkrumah was arrested — and the Gold Coast did not forget Christiansborg Crossroads.

lines.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
lines.text: They thought fear would end the movement. It only taught us where to stand.

lines.speaker: Yaw
lines.text: My yard still stands. So does my grandfather's debt — paid in witnesses, not in silence.

lines.speaker: Kojo
lines.text: Adjetey. Attipoe. Lamptey. We did not save them. We carried what they died proving.

lines.speaker: Kwesi
lines.text: Every page we filed became evidence. Every witness became a country.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: On the sixth of March, nineteen fifty-seven, the Gold Coast became Ghana — free at last.

lines.speaker: Narrator
lines.text: Sergeant Adjetey, Corporal Attipoe, Private Odartey Lamptey — martyrs of the Crossroads. Their names outlived the Empire.

1.missionTarget: Collect the ledgers — prove the Crown owes veterans.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: Kingsway Depot before dawn. Lamptey — side gate. Attipoe — rear cover.

start.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
start.text: Three million pounds the Treasury denies. Bring me the ledgers before they burn them.

start.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
start.text: Copy that. We move quiet — constables rotate at the hour mark.

start.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
start.text: If they torch the records, we march on memory alone. Not today.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: Three ledgers. Extraction east. Ink before iron — always.

mid.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
mid.text: Guard turning the corner — ten seconds, Sergeant!

mid.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
mid.text: Second ledger under the floorboard. They almost burned it.

victory.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
victory.text: Every page accounted for. They can't say we weren't owed.

victory.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
victory.text: Evidence secured. The debt is public now — whether Creasy reads it or not.

victory.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
victory.text: Accounts, not charity. The movement has its first witness.

asareStart.speaker: Kofi Asare
asareStart.text: I clerk nights at Kingsway. Quick — before the guard returns.

asareStart.speaker: Kofi Asare
asareStart.text: Side gate is open. Trust me on this one, Sergeant.

Ledger A.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
Ledger A.text: Three million pounds owed — the Governor's office denies the figure.

Ledger A.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
Ledger A.text: They denied it in public. The ledger doesn't lie.

Ledger B.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
Ledger B.text: Two thousand Union members in Accra alone. That's an army of witnesses.

Ledger B.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
Ledger B.text: File it. Every name is a vote they can't count.

Ledger C.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
Ledger C.text: Colonial Secretary orders all benefit records classified by month's end.

Ledger C.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
Ledger C.text: They're burning the trail. We beat them to the ash.

2.missionTarget: Escort organizers to the safe house — protect the petition draft.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: Kaneshie awning — UGCC organizers under the market cloth. We escort, we don't preach.

start.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
start.text: Constabulary has two posts on the north lane. We cut east if pressed.

start.speaker: Organizer
start.text: The petition draft is in the satchel. If we fall, the words must not.

start.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
start.text: Market women know our faces. Keep the VIPs between us and the road.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: Four souls minimum reach the safe house. No rifles — only cover.

mid.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
mid.text: North post is stirring — keep the awning between us and their line of sight.

mid.speaker: Organizer
mid.text: Bonne's boycott closed half these stalls. They still send constables after words.

victory.speaker: Organizer
victory.text: You brought us through the market alive. Nkrumah will hear of this.

victory.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
victory.text: Safe house secured. The petition lives another day.

victory.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
victory.text: Discipline in the market is discipline on the march. Well done.

asareStart.speaker: Kofi Asare
asareStart.text: Forged curfew pass — show this if stopped. Trust me.

asareStart.speaker: Kofi Asare
asareStart.text: The north lane is hot tonight. East cut is clear for twenty minutes.

onExtraction.speaker: UGCC Organizer
onExtraction.text: We owe you more than thanks. Self-government is not a slogan anymore — it's a schedule.

onExtraction.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
onExtraction.text: Rest tonight. Tomorrow the coast road watches everything we do.

3.missionTarget: Blind colonial radio — plant both masts and hold the fuse.

start.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
start.text: Two masts, two charges. Alpha first — Beta from the blind side.

start.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
start.text: Hold the arming zone or the fuse dies with us.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: Lamptey — how long on the fuse?

start.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
start.text: Fifteen seconds if I do my job. Don't ask me to do it twice.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: Steel falls, we run. No heroics on the ridge.

mid.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
mid.text: Alpha's wired. Attipoe — when Beta's set, we run together.

mid.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
mid.text: Beta's hot. Lamptey, you're frighteningly good at this.

mid.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
mid.text: Burma taught me what steel sounds like when it falls.

victory.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
victory.text: Radio Hill silent. Colonial troop movements go blind tomorrow.

victory.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
victory.text: Both masts down. They march deaf into the twenty-eighth.

victory.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
victory.text: Ridge clear. Extraction west — move before they count the wreckage.

asareStart.speaker: Kofi Asare
asareStart.text: Patrol shift at zero-four-hundred — note on your crate. Accurate intel.

asareStart.speaker: Kofi Asare
asareStart.text: South patrol loops late. You have a window — use it.

onFirstDemolition.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
onFirstDemolition.text: First mast wired. One more — then we run like the hill is on fire.

onHoldStart.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
onHoldStart.text: Hold this ground — Lamptey arms the fuse. Nobody crosses the circle.

onHoldStart.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
onHoldStart.text: DEFEND formation. Fifteen seconds is a lifetime under fire.

4.missionTarget: Clear James Town checkpoint — fish crates carry the pamphlets.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: James Town checkpoint — fish crates, not rifles. Clear one hour for the pamphlets.

start.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
start.text: Fishermen are waiting on the beach road. Don't spook the cart.

start.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
start.text: Pamphlets under the ice. Constables search for guns — we give them fish.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: Three guards down, cart through, coast road open. One hour.

mid.speaker: Fisherman
mid.text: Patrol boat on the horizon — you have minutes, not an hour!

mid.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
mid.text: Crates through now. Bonne's boycott taught them to read — let's feed them pages.

victory.speaker: Fisherman
victory.text: Crates through. The coast will read what the Castle won't.

victory.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
victory.text: Checkpoint open. Pamphlets flow — quietly.

victory.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
victory.text: Sealed orders confirm it — peaceful march to Christiansborg. File that.

asareStart.speaker: Kofi Asare
asareStart.text: Special patrol tonight — so they say. It never arrives. Was I wrong, Sergeant?

asareStart.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
asareStart.text: …We'll see who's wrong when the crates are through.

Sealed Orders.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
Sealed Orders.text: Petition will be presented at Christiansborg — peaceful march, unarmed.

Sealed Orders.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
Sealed Orders.text: Even their sealed orders admit we're not rebels. Remember that at the gate.

5.missionTarget: Capture the courier alive — read what Creasy signed.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: Ring Road intercept. Wound the courier — seal intact. We need what Creasy signed.

start.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
start.text: One shot to the leg if you must. Not the chest.

start.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
start.text: Dead courier, dead intelligence. We need his satchel breathing.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: Capture, deliver to Asare at the handoff. Watch what he does with the pages.

mid.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
mid.text: Courier's bleeding but running — leg shot only, Sergeant!

mid.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
mid.text: Satchel intact or we learn nothing. Corner him at the wall.

victory.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
victory.text: Orders intercepted — but one page is missing. Asare's eyes didn't meet mine.

victory.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
victory.text: Something doesn't add up. Keep the satchel close.

onHandoff.speaker: Kofi Asare
onHandoff.text: Satchel received. One page is missing — you noticed.

onHandoff.speaker: Kofi Asare
onHandoff.text: …Not everything I copy goes to the same desk. Not yet.

onHandoff.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
onHandoff.text: Trust frays on missing ink. I file that too.

Partial Orders.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
Partial Orders.text: …maintain order by all necessary means… rest redacted — page missing.

Partial Orders.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
Partial Orders.text: Necessary means. That's the word that keeps me awake.

6.missionTarget: Survive Makola ambush — save the press cart and Attipoe.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: Makola corridor — Asare's route. Move the press plates before curfew.

start.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
start.text: Cart stays between us. Lamptey — rear guard.

start.speaker: Kofi Asare
start.text: East corridor is marked safe on my map. Move now — curfew in forty minutes.

start.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
start.text: Plates are heavy. So is trust. We move anyway.

mid.speaker: Colonial Radio
mid.text: Wrong corridor — false corridor. Intercept at Makola east.

mid.speaker: Kofi Asare
mid.text: Only I knew this route… Sergeant, I did not call that in.

mid.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
mid.text: Attipoe — get the cart out! Lamptey — suppressing fire!

victory.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
victory.text: Still breathing. Barely. Cart saved.

victory.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
victory.text: Asare's name burns in my memory — betrayal or misdirection, we'll know.

victory.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
victory.text: March date's on the intel we pulled — twenty-eighth, Christ Church. Plates live.

ambush.speaker: Colonial Radio
ambush.text: Wrong corridor — false corridor. Intercept at Makola east.

ambush.speaker: Kofi Asare
ambush.text: Only Asare knew this route. Sergeant — I swear on the petition I didn't—

ambush.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
ambush.text: Save the speech. Save the cart.

onWoundAttipoe.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
onWoundAttipoe.text: Round through the shoulder — still fighting! Don't stop for me!

onWoundAttipoe.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
onWoundAttipoe.text: Attipoe takes a hit — still moving. Get the cart out!

7.missionTarget: Shadow Asare — witness whose desk he truly serves.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: I no longer know whose desk Asare serves. Shadow him — don't alert the Castle gate.

start.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
start.text: Stay within a hundred twenty paces. Close enough to see, far enough to deny.

start.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
start.text: If he's colonial, we end it at the gate. If he's ours, we march blind no more.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: Witness the exchange. Press E when you see the handoff. No shots unless they fire first.

mid.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
mid.text: He's burning a map — real or decoy?

mid.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
mid.text: Don't break cover. If he's Gold Coast, he'll prove it when it costs him.

victory.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
victory.text: Some lies protect true marchers. Asare was always Gold Coast.

victory.speaker: Kofi Asare
victory.text: I burned the real troop map. The sergeant got the fake. Trust restored — barely.

victory.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
victory.text: The Clerk serves the Coast. We march with clearer eyes.

witness.speaker: Witness
witness.text: Asare burns a real map — hands a fake to the colonial sergeant.

witness.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
witness.text: Some lies protect true marchers. Remember that at the Crossroads.

8.missionTarget: Hold Christ Church — hear the petition read aloud.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: Christ Church — dawn. Thousands gather. Hold the square until the petition is read.

start.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
start.text: Speakers on the steps. We protect — we do not provoke.

start.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
start.text: Two thousand unarmed veterans. Let the world see discipline before it sees iron.

start.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
start.text: Forty-five seconds on the steps. Waves will come — hold the square.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: Ink before iron. Protect the speakers. Let them read every word.

mid.speaker: Speaker
mid.text: We march today with empty hands and full hearts — loyal subjects demanding justice!

mid.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
mid.text: Hold the line — constables are testing the edges. Do not give them cause.

victory.speaker: Crowd
victory.text: Self-government now! Self-government now!

victory.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
victory.text: Petition read. The crowd swells toward the coast road.

victory.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
victory.text: You held the square. Now hold your nerve on the march.

asareStart.speaker: Wanted Poster
asareStart.text: Kofi Asare — reward for arrest. He is gone. For now.

asareStart.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
asareStart.text: Clerk's face on every wall. He knew this was coming.

onHoldStart.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
onHoldStart.text: Hold the church steps — forty-five seconds. Speakers stay alive.

onHoldStart.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
onHoldStart.text: DEFEND the square. Civilians behind us.

Petition Read.speaker: Speaker
Petition Read.text: We, the ex-servicemen of the Gold Coast, present this petition in peace and loyalty—

Petition Read.speaker: Crowd
Petition Read.text: …demanding self-government now! Not next year. Now!

Petition Read.speaker: Speaker
Petition Read.text: We march unarmed. Let the Governor hear us — or let the world hear his silence.

9.missionTarget: Escort the unarmed column to the Crossroads approach.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: Coast road column. Unarmed. Disciplined. Visible. The world is watching.

start.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
start.text: Escorts in formation. No weapons among us — only dignity.

start.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
start.text: Three waypoints. Petition bearer stays center. We are witnesses with boots.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: Nkrumah's voice on the radio at the second bend. Discipline is the weapon today.

mid.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
mid.text: Comrades — the Castle wall is ahead. Discipline is your weapon today.

mid.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
mid.text: Crossroads in sight. Eyes up. We present the petition — we do not provoke.

victory.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
victory.text: Crossroads approach. The Castle wall ahead. History holds its breath.

victory.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
victory.text: You marched as I asked. Whatever comes at the gate — you did not strike first.

radio.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
radio.text: Comrades — discipline is your weapon today. Ink before iron.

radio.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
radio.text: Hold the line with dignity. Let them be the ones who strike first.

onWaypoint.speaker: Kofi Asare
onWaypoint.text: Smoke signal on the Castle wall — my mark. Gate counts were falsified.

onWaypoint.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
onWaypoint.text: He still serves us. Eyes up — Crossroads ahead.

10.missionTarget: Document the Crossroads — extract witnesses, not rewrite history.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: Christiansborg Crossroads. Present the petition. Do not provoke.

start.speaker: Superintendent Imray
start.text: Halt! You will not pass. Disperse or face consequences.

start.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
start.text: We are unarmed. We demand the Governor receive our petition.

start.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
start.text: Seventeen constables at the gate. Document everything — press E at each witness point.

start.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
start.text: We cannot stop what comes. We can make sure the world reads it true.

mid.speaker: Witness
mid.text: They're loading — Imray's giving the order!

mid.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
mid.text: Get the witnesses out — document what happens here!

mid.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
mid.text: You cannot save every name today — save the truth.

victory.speaker: Narrator
victory.text: Accra will remember. The Watson Commission will come.

victory.speaker: Dr. Kwame Nkrumah
victory.text: Independence is inevitable — not today, but because of today.

victory.speaker: Witness
victory.text: Three martyrs at the gate. The petition never reached Creasy — but the truth escaped.

witness1.speaker: Witness
witness1.text: Unarmed veterans at the gate — I see no weapons among the marchers.

witness1.speaker: Private Odartey Lamptey
witness1.text: Witness one filed. Keep moving — more eyes needed.

witness2.speaker: Witness
witness2.text: Superintendent Imray's constables open fire without warning!

witness2.speaker: Sergeant Adjetey
witness2.text: Document it. Do not look away.

witness3.speaker: Witness
witness3.text: The petition never reached Governor Creasy at the Castle.

witness3.speaker: Corporal Attipoe
witness3.text: Truth out — now extract whoever still breathes.

witness.speaker: Kofi Asare
witness.text: They'll arrest me at dawn. The petition copy is safe. I was always Gold Coast.

11.missionTarget: Slip the Makola cordon — reach James Town before dusk closes.

start.speaker: Kojo
start.text: Crossroads dust on my boots. Constables sweep Makola — we slip alleys, not main roads.

start.speaker: Araba
start.text: Salaga tonight. I know a fishmonger who won't swallow what Imray did.

start.speaker: Kojo
start.text: They want a riot in the open. I give them geometry in the dark.

start.speaker: Araba
start.text: Cousin Kwame lost a leg today. AWAM raised prices while he bled. Move.

mid.speaker: Kojo
mid.text: Cordon tightening — ninety seconds to the drainage cut!

mid.speaker: Araba
mid.text: Constable pair on the corner — I can break their line or we double back.

victory.speaker: Kojo
victory.text: James Town exit clear. Salaga Market — find Araba's contact before the cordon closes.

victory.speaker: Yaw
victory.text: Radio crackle from my yard: all four still breathing. Don't waste it.

cellStart.speaker: Kojo
cellStart.text: They expect a riot. I give them a route.

cellStart.speaker: Araba
cellStart.text: Riot breaks windows. Routes break cordons. Follow the boy who counts alleys.

12.missionTarget: Hit AWAM's store — protect innocent stalls.

start.speaker: Araba
start.text: AWAM shuttered their store — hired constables to guard hunger. We hit the warehouse.

start.speaker: Kojo
start.text: Not Auntie Esi's fish table. Yaw's rule — every time.

start.speaker: Kofi
start.text: I'll cover the stalls. I know which awning is innocent.

start.speaker: Araba
start.text: Cousin bleeds from Crossroads. AWAM still sells at monopoly prices. Tonight that ends.

start.speaker: Kojo
start.text: Demolish the store front. Hold the square. Stalls stay standing.

mid.speaker: Araba
mid.text: You shot the men who wore your khaki. You do not get to sell us hunger anymore.

mid.speaker: Market Woman
mid.text: AWAM's shutters fall — our prices stay honest!

victory.speaker: Araba
victory.text: AWAM burns. Kaneshie smokes at dawn — a boy runs toward the fire.

victory.speaker: Kojo
victory.text: That's Kofi before we pulled him out. Next mission — we don't leave him standing still.

cellStart.speaker: Araba
cellStart.text: You shot the men who wore your khaki. You do not get to sell us hunger anymore.

cellStart.speaker: Yaw
cellStart.text: Hit the monopoly — not the market women. My grandfather's rule stands.

onFirstDemolition.speaker: Araba
onFirstDemolition.text: AWAM shutters down! Constables at the warehouse — hold the square!

onFirstDemolition.speaker: Kojo
onFirstDemolition.text: Stalls stay alive. AWAM doesn't. Thirty seconds — move!

onHoldStart.speaker: Kojo
onHoldStart.text: Hold Salaga square — protect the stall VIPs. AWAM hired these constables.

onHoldStart.speaker: Kofi
onHoldStart.text: I know these awnings. Innocent tables stay standing — I swear it.

start.speaker: Kojo
start.text: First riot fires at Kaneshie AWAM warehouse. Pull Kofi from the constables — drainage cut east.

start.speaker: Araba
start.text: Constables at the corner. Grab his arm, not his chest.

mid.speaker: Kofi
mid.text: I didn't know how to hold a rifle yesterday. I will learn today.

mid.speaker: Kojo
mid.text: You run today. You learn tomorrow. Move on my count.

victory.speaker: Kofi
victory.text: I ran when you said run. I won't stand still again.

victory.speaker: Yaw
victory.text: Ussher Town yard tonight. Roofs, tools, and rules — if you make it alive.

cellStart.speaker: Kofi
cellStart.text: I didn't know how to hold a rifle yesterday. I will learn today.

start.speaker: Kwesi
start.text: Don't step into the light. Satchel has four pages — patrol times, keys, Imray's signature.

start.speaker: Kojo
start.text: You read the rooms. We move on what you know. No firing — that's your rule.

start.speaker: Kwesi
start.text: And when they catch me?

start.speaker: Kojo
start.text: We come. All of us.

victory.speaker: Kwesi
victory.text: Every manifest accounted for. Truth is ammunition.

victory.speaker: Kojo
victory.text: The cell swears its chain at Yaw's yard. Kofi — you shoot straighter tomorrow.

cellStart.speaker: Kwesi
cellStart.text: Sorry doesn't read curfew times. This does.

start.speaker: Yaw
start.text: Fifth day. Constables sweep Ussher Town for riot organizers. Hold my yard.

start.speaker: Kojo
start.text: All four survive — or we don't come back. Watson Commission word must reach the coast.

start.speaker: Araba
start.text: Let them try the front gate.

mid.speaker: Kwesi
mid.text: Witness accounts filed — AWAM price-fixing, Imray's suppression order.

mid.speaker: Kofi
mid.text: Innocent stalls still standing. AWAM warehouses didn't.

victory.speaker: Yaw
victory.text: Four children and an old man's yard — still standing.

victory.speaker: Kojo
victory.text: Accra burned five days. We burned with purpose.

victory.speaker: Narrator
victory.text: The Watson Commission will come. Nkrumah will be arrested. Independence: 1957.

cellStart.speaker: Yaw
cellStart.text: Four children and an old man's yard. Independence is not this week — but the path starts here.
